questions.text: Statements: I. Standard of living among the middle class society is constantly going up since part of few years. II. The Pakistani economy is observing remarkable growth.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I suggests an improvement in the standard of living, which could lead to economic growth as a result of increased consumption and investment.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Economic growth can lead to an improved standard of living, but the statements as presented do not directly support this causal relationship.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: If both statements were independent causes, they would not influence each other, which contradicts the logical relationship between economic growth and standard of living.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Considering the logical relationship, it is more plausible to consider one statement as the cause of the other rather than both being effects of separate causes.

questions.explanation: Remarkable growth in the national economy (Statement II) is the cause. This growth leads to increased incomes and opportunities, which in turn results in an improved standard of living for the middle class (Statement I).

questions.text: l.The car manufacturing companies have recently increased the prices of mid-sized cars. ll. The Government recently increased the duty on mid-sized cars.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option suggests that the increase in car prices led to the government increasing the duty. This is illogical as government policy changes are not typically reactions to market price changes.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
options.explanation: This option implies that a single external factor caused both the increase in car prices and the increased duty. However, the statements do not provide evidence of such a common cause.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: This option suggests that the increase in car prices and the increased duty occurred independently. However, the logical relationship indicates that one directly affects the other.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: This option correctly identifies that the government's decision to increase the duty on mid-sized cars (Statement II) led to car manufacturing companies raising their prices (Statement I) due to increased costs.

questions.explanation: In this logical reasoning question, we are examining the cause and effect relationship between the two statements. Statement II states that the government has increased the duty on mid-sized cars, which would logically increase production costs for car manufacturers. In response to this increased cost, car manufacturers would raise the prices of mid-sized cars, as indicated in Statement I.Therefore, Statement II (government increased duty) is the cause, and Statement I (car manufacturers increased prices) is the effect.Other options are incorrect because they either suggest that the price increase caused the duty increase, both are effects of a common cause, or both occurred independently, none of which align with the logical progression from cause to effect in the given statements.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes.Statement: There is a considerable reduction in the number of people affected by water-borne diseases in city A during this rainy season. The government has opened four new civil hospitals in City A at the beginning of the year.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is wrong because I is not the cause and II is not it's effect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is wrong because II is not the cause and I is not its effect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Effect: Reduction in water-borne diseases (Statement I)Possible cause: Opening hospitals (Statement II)Opening hospitals (Statement II) may help in treatment, but the reduction in water-borne diseases also depends on water quality, sanitation, rainfall, etc. → independent causeThe reduction itself (Statement I) is actually the effect, not a cause.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is wrong because I and II are not effects of independent causes. They are independent causes.

questions.explanation: Effect: Reduction in water-borne diseases (Statement I)Possible cause: Opening hospitals (Statement II)Opening hospitals (Statement II) may help in treatment, but the reduction in water-borne diseases also depends on water quality, sanitation, rainfall, etc. → independent causeThe reduction itself (Statement I) is actually the effect, not a cause.

questions.text: Statement: There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year. The government has decided to increase the import quantum of edible oil.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Option A is correct because , there is sharp decline in production of oilseed this year and because of this the government has decided to increase import quantum of edible oil. As option A is the only correct option so all other options are incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect (Option b):Option (b) suggests that the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil (Statement II) is the cause, and the sharp decline in oilseed production (Statement I) is the effect. This option implies that the government's decision to increase imports somehow resulted in the decline in oilseed production.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both the statements I and II are independent causes (Option c):Option (c) suggests that both the decline in the production of oilseeds and the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil are independent causes and not directly related to each other.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: d) Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause (Option d):Option (d) suggests that both the decline in the production of oilseeds and the government's decision to increase the import quantum of edible oil are effects of some common cause, meaning there is an underlying reason or factor that influenced both situations.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct because , there is sharp decline in production of oilseed this year and because of this the government has decided to increase import quantum of edible oil. As option A is the only correct option so all other options are incorrect.

questions.text: Dependent cause/ Independent causes:I. The construction of dams has created an obstruction in the migration of animals. II. The animals of Amazon have turned into ferocious beasts, that are damaging the property as well as a threat to the lives of people.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Incorrect. The construction of dams causing obstructions does not explain why animals would become ferocious.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Incorrect. The aggressive behavior of animals does not lead to the construction of dams.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Incorrect. While the statements could be independent, the question does not support this conclusion as per the provided statements.

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: Correct. There is no causal relationship or independent cause link between the two statements. Statement I discusses the environmental impact of dams, while Statement II describes animal behavior without a direct link to the dams.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'None of these options are correct' because there is no causal relationship between the two statements. Statement I refers to the environmental impact of dam construction on animal migration, while Statement II describes a behavioral change in animals without any clear link to dam construction. Therefore, neither statement can be considered to be a cause or effect of the other.

questions.text: I. Since Amna’s baby died at birth, she wanted another immediately.II. Amna abducted an infant and took it out of the hospital.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: Amna's grief over her baby's death (Statement I) led to her decision to abduct another baby (Statement II). Thus, Statement I is the cause and Statement II is the effect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because abducting a baby (Statement II) does not cause the death of Amna's own baby or her desire for another.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: This is incorrect as the statements are not independent; the emotional state in Statement I leads to the action in Statement II.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because there is a direct link between the two statements where one leads to the other, not a separate cause for each.

questions.explanation: In logical reasoning, understanding cause and effect involves recognizing the sequence of events where one event directly leads to another. Here, the death of Amna's baby at birth (Statement I) is the catalyst for her grief and subsequent actions. Her intense desire for another child, driven by this grief, results in her decision to abduct an infant from the hospital (Statement II). Hence, Statement I is the cause, and Statement II is the effect. The other options are incorrect because they either reverse the cause-effect relationship or incorrectly assume independence between the statements.

questions.text: Dependent /Independent causes: Statement I: There is an unprecedented increase in the number of young unemployed in comparison to the previous year. Statement II: A large number of candidates submitted applications against an advertisement for the post of manager issued by a bank.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the cause-effect relationship between the two statements is the opposite of what is stated in option (b). As explained above, Statement I (increase in young unemployed) is the cause, and Statement II (large number of applications for the managerial position) is its effect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the statements are not independent causes. There is a cause-effect relationship between the two statements.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the statements are not independent effects either. The two statements are related, with Statement I (increase in young unemployed) being the cause, and Statement II (large number of applications for the managerial position) being its effect.

questions.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

questions.text: Statement:I. Large number of Primary Schools in rural areas are run by only one teacher.II. There has been a huge dropout from primary schools in rural areas.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause then 2 is its effect
options.explanation: One teacher running a lot of schools leads to mismanagement and it lowers the educational standard which causes students to drop out from school in rural areas. So, option A is the most accurate answer.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause then I is its effect
options.explanation: Option B (Statement 2 is the cause, then I is its effect) is incorrect because the dropout from primary schools in rural areas can have various causes, and there is no indication that it directly causes the large number of schools run by one teacher.

options.text: Both statements are independent causes
options.explanation: Option C (Both statements are independent causes) is incorrect because the two statements do not appear to have any direct connection or correlation. They describe different situations without any clear causal relationship.

options.text: Both of the statements are effect of independent causes
options.explanation: Option D (Both of the statements are effects of independent causes) is incorrect because there is no evidence provided to suggest that both statements are effects of distinct, unrelated causes. Again, they seem to describe different aspects of primary education in rural areas without any evident causal link.

questions.explanation: One teacher running a lot of schools leads to mismanagement and it lowers the educational standard which causes students to drop out from school in rural areas. So, option A is the most accurate answer.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. Stefan's English-speaking power is very less.II. Stefan has decided to join Saint Pants English coaching class.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: Stefan's limited English-speaking skills could logically lead him to join an English coaching class to improve his abilities, making statement I the cause and statement II the effect.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: Joining an English coaching class (statement II) typically occurs because of the need to improve English-speaking skills, not the other way around. Therefore, statement II cannot be the cause of statement I.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: There is a logical relationship between the two statements; Stefan's decision to join a class is not independent of his English-speaking skills.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: Stefan's decision to join a class (statement II) directly stems from his need to improve his English-speaking skills (statement I), so they are not effects of independent causes.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;
options.explanation: While it might seem possible that a common cause like a job requirement could lead to both statements, the direct relationship suggests a clearer cause-effect link between statements I and II.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A. Stefan's limited English-speaking skills (statement I) logically lead him to join an English coaching class (statement II) to improve his proficiency. Therefore, statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect. Option B is incorrect because joining a class does not cause one's English-speaking skills to be low. Option C is incorrect as the statements are not independent; they are related. Option D is incorrect because the statements are not effects of independent causes; they are directly related. Finally, option E is incorrect because although a common cause could be possible, the direct link between the two statements is more evident.

questions.text: Verbal ReasoningStatements:(I) He has got cavities in his tooth (II) He eats too many chocolates
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement 1 is the cause and statement 2 is its effect.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because having cavities cannot cause someone to eat chocolates.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause and statement 1 is its effect.
options.explanation: This is correct because excessive consumption of chocolates, which are high in sugar, can lead to cavities due to bacterial growth and acid production in the mouth.

options.text: Both statements are independent causes.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the two statements are related, with one leading to the other.

options.text: Both statements are of a common cause.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the statements do not share a common cause; rather, one is the cause and the other is the effect.

questions.explanation: Statement 2 is the cause and statement 1 is its effect. Eating too many chocolates is a known cause of cavities due to the sugar content, which promotes bacterial growth and acid production. As a result, this leads to tooth decay and cavities. Statement 1 being the cause would not logically lead to statement 2. Additionally, the statements are not independent as they are directly related, nor do they share a common cause.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes:Statement: There is an unprecedented increase in the number of young unemployed in comparison to the previous year. A large number of candidates submitted applications against an advertisement for the post of manager issued by a bank.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effects
options.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effectThis option suggests that the increase in youth unemployment (Statement I) is causing more young people to apply for jobs (Statement II). This makes sense because if more young people are unemployed, they are likely to seek out job opportunities, as described in Statement II. Therefore, this option is correct.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effectThis option suggests that the large number of applications (Statement II) is causing the increase in youth unemployment (Statement I). However, this doesn't make sense logically. Typically, an increase in applications would indicate a demand for jobs, not necessarily an increase in unemployment. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both the statements I and II are independent causesThis option suggests that both statements are separate causes for something else. But Statement II seems to be directly related to the unemployment issue (as people are applying for jobs due to being unemployed). So, these are not independent causes either. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causesThis option suggests that both statements are caused by separate, independent factors. However, Statement II could be seen as a reaction to the job market, influenced by the unemployment situation described in Statement I. So, these are not independent causes; rather, Statement II is related to Statement I. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effectThis option suggests that the increase in youth unemployment (Statement I) is causing more young people to apply for jobs (Statement II). This makes sense because if more young people are unemployed, they are likely to seek out job opportunities, as described in Statement II. Therefore, this option is correct.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes:Statement: Most of the steel-producing companies in the country have made a considerable profit during the last financial year. Many Asian countries have been importing huge quantities of steel from Pakistan.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the profit made by steel companies is not the cause but the result of increased exports.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is the correct option. The increased imports of steel from Pakistan by Asian countries led to a profit for the steel-producing companies.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect as the two statements are directly related, with one causing the other.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the profit and the importation are not effects of separate causes; they are directly linked.

questions.explanation: Statement II is the cause as it describes the action of many Asian countries importing steel from Pakistan. This increase in demand led to a significant profit for the steel-producing companies, which is described in Statement I. Therefore, Statement II is the cause, and Statement I is its effect. The other options are incorrect because they either reverse the cause-effect relationship or suggest a lack of connection between the statements, which is not the case.

questions.text: Statements: I. The university authorities have instructed all colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of cell phones inside the college premises. II. Majority of the teachers of the college signed a joint petition to the university complaining the disturbances caused by cell phone ring tones inside the classrooms.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the instruction from the university authority is a response to a complaint, not the cause of it.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The complaint made by the teachers about disturbances caused by cell phones led the university authority to instruct colleges to ban cell phones.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect as the statements are related; one is the cause and the other is the effect, not independent causes.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because both statements are not effects of separate causes; rather, one is a cause and the other is its effect.

questions.explanation: The petition and complaints from the teachers (Statement II) provided the reason and impetus for the university authorities to take action. The resulting ban on cell phones (Statement I) is the effect of these complaints.

questions.text: Statements: I. The police authorities have recently increased vigil during the evening hours in the locality. II. There has been considerable reduction in the incidents of petty crimes in the locality.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: The increased police vigilance is directly responsible for the reduction in petty crimes, establishing a clear cause and effect relationship.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This interpretation is incorrect because the reduction in petty crimes is not likely to cause increased police vigilance; rather, it's typically the other way around.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements are related, with one being the cause and the other being the effect, rather than independent causes.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This is incorrect because statement I is an action that leads directly to the outcome described in statement II, rather than both being effects of separate causes.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
options.explanation: This is not correct as both statements are directly linked as cause and effect rather than having a common cause.

questions.explanation: Increased police presence and vigilance (Statement I) is a preventative measure designed to deter crime. A reduction in crime (Statement II) is the logical and intended effect of such a measure.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. The fanners have decided against selling their kharif crops to the Government agencies.II. The Government has reduced the procurement price of kharif crops starting from last month to the next six months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: This option suggests that the decision not to sell crops led to the reduction in procurement price. This is unlikely as the decision to not sell is a response, not a cause.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: This option correctly identifies that the Government's decision to reduce procurement prices could lead to farmers deciding against selling their crops to government agencies.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This option suggests that each statement has its own cause unrelated to the other. Given the context, this is unlikely as the reduction in price directly affects the farmers' decision.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This option implies that both statements result from separate causes. However, the reduction in procurement price directly influences the farmers' decision, making them connected.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: This option suggests that there is a common cause leading to both statements. While plausible, the direct causal relationship between the two statements is more evident.

questions.explanation: Statement II describes a governmental action of reducing procurement prices for kharif crops, which could naturally lead to Statement I, where farmers decide against selling to government agencies due to unfavorable prices. Therefore, Statement II is the cause, and Statement I is its effect. Other options are incorrect because they either misinterpret the causal relationship or suggest independence or a common cause that is not supported by the given information.

questions.text: Statements: I. The Government has imported large quantities of sugar as per the trade agreements with other countries. II. The prices of sugar in the domestic market have fallen sharply in recent months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II Is its effect
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. In economics, when the government imports a large quantity of a commodity (sugar), it significantly increases the total supply available in the domestic market. According to the law of supply and demand, an increase in supply, while demand remains constant, leads to a sharp decrease in price. Therefore, the act of importing (Statement I) is the direct cause of the falling prices (Statement II).

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because governments typically import goods to combat high prices or shortages. Falling prices in the domestic market would reduce the need for imports rather than causing them.

options.text: Both statements are independent causes
options.explanation: This is incorrect because there is a clear, direct functional link between the arrival of new stock (imports) and the subsequent change in market value. They are part of the same economic chain.

options.text: Both statements are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This is incorrect because Statement I explicitly describes an action taken (importing), which then serves as the primary driver for the outcome described in Statement II.

questions.explanation: According to the economic principle of supply and demand, importing large quantities of a commodity (Statement I) increases its supply. An increase in supply, with demand remaining constant, is a direct cause for a decrease in market prices (Statement II).

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes:I. The student was expelled from the school. II. He was found using the drugs.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: a) Statement I is the cause, and Statement II is its effect.This option suggests that Statement I (The student was expelled from the school) is the cause, and Statement II (He was found using drugs) is the effect of that cause. In other words, the student's expulsion from school led to the discovery that he was using drugs.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: b) Statement II is the cause, and Statement I is its effect.This option suggests the opposite of option a), stating that Statement II (He was found using drugs) is the cause, and Statement I (The student was expelled from the school) is the effect. In this interpretation, the discovery of drug use resulted in the student's expulsion from school.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both statements I and II are independent causes.This option suggests that both events (the student's expulsion and the discovery of drug use) are independent of each other, meaning they are not related as cause and effect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.
options.explanation: d) Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause.This option implies that there is another independent cause that led to both events happening. In this case, the expulsion and the discovery of drug use are outcomes resulting from a common cause.

questions.explanation: The school probably has a no drug policy so when the school found the person with drugs they expelled him.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. She got admitted into one of the top colleges.II. She worked day and night to achieve a good score in the entrance exam.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because admission to a top college (Statement I) cannot be the cause of working hard for an entrance exam (Statement II).

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: This option is correct. Statement II describes the effort and dedication to achieve a good score in the entrance exam, which is the cause of her getting admitted into a top college as mentioned in Statement I.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the statements are not independent; they are related through a cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the statements are not effects of independent causes. Statement II is the cause of Statement I.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is no common cause affecting both statements. Instead, Statement II directly leads to Statement I.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect. Statement II describes the hard work and dedication to achieve a good score in the entrance exam, which likely resulted in her admission to one of the top colleges, as stated in Statement I. The other options are incorrect because they either suggest independent relationships or common causes, which are not applicable in this scenario.

questions.text: Statement I: The PIC doctors are on a strike.Statement II: There was a death in PIC due to non-availability of the doctor for the check-up.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: As the doctors were on strike, so they were not available in the hospital. Due to the absence of doctors , there was a death.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: It is incorrect option. As the doctors were on strike, so they were not available in the hospital. Due to the absence of doctors , there was a death.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: It is the incorrect option. As the doctors were on strike, so they were not available in the hospital. Due to the absence of doctors , there was a death.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of an independent cause
options.explanation: It is the incorrect option. As the doctors were on strike, so they were not available in the hospital. Due to the absence of doctors , there was a death.

questions.explanation: As the doctors were on strike, so they were not available in the hospital. Due to the absence of doctors , there was a death.

questions.text: Statements: I. The committee appointed by the Government on the fee structure of the professional courses has drastically reduced the fees of various courses in comparison to those charged in the last year.II. The parents of aspiring students seeking admission to professional courses had launched a severe agitation protesting against the high fees charged by the professional institutes and the admission process was delayed considerably.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. If Statement I were the cause, the sequence of events would imply that the reduction in fees led to the agitation, which does not logically follow.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is correct. The severe agitation by the parents led the government to appoint a committee to review and reduce the fees, demonstrating a clear cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: Both Statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The statements are not independent as there is a logical connection between them; the agitation prompted the government's intervention.

options.text: Both Statements I and II are effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The statements are related, with one being a cause and the other an effect, rather than both being effects of separate causes.

options.text: Both Statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While both statements involve the fee issue, Statement II clearly leads to Statement I, rather than both resulting from a third, common cause.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect. The agitation by the parents against high fees put pressure on the government to take action, resulting in the appointment of a committee that reduced the fees. Option A is incorrect because the reduction in fees did not cause the agitation. Options C, D, and E are incorrect because they either view the statements as independent or as effects of a common cause, neither of which accurately reflects the sequence and relationship of the events described.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes.Statement: There is a considerable reduction in the number of people affected by water-borne diseases in city A during this rainy season. The government has opened four new civil hospitals in City A at the beginning of the year.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Effect: Reduction in water-borne diseases (Statement I)Possible cause: Opening hospitals (Statement II)Opening hospitals (Statement II) may help in treatment, but the reduction in water-borne diseases also depends on water quality, sanitation, rainfall, etc. → independent causeThe reduction itself (Statement I) is actually the effect, not a cause.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is wrong because I and II are not effects of independent causes. They are independent causes.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: The two statements are unrelated and independent of each other. The two statements might be independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. Majority of the students in the college expressed their Opinion against the college authority's decision to break away from the university and become autonomous.II. The university authorities have expressed their inability to provide grants to its constituent colleges.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If statement I is the cause and statemerit ats effect;
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The students' opinion against autonomy does not cause the university's inability to provide grants.

options.text: If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect:
options.explanation: This is correct. The university's inability to provide grants could lead to the college seeking autonomy, which in turn causes student opposition.

options.text: If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The statements are not independent; they are related through the issue of autonomy and financial support.

options.text: If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The statements are not effects of independent causes; they are linked by the financial decisions of the university.

options.text: If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There is a direct causal link between the two statements, not just a common underlying cause.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The university's inability to provide grants (Statement II) is likely a reason for the college's decision to seek autonomy, which in turn leads to the students' opposition (Statement I). Therefore, Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect. Other options are incorrect as they do not accurately capture the causal relationship between the statements.

questions.text: Statements: I. Ms. Kulsoom labelled Mr. Waleed as a deserter. II. Ms. Kulsoom offered an apology.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: There is a strong cause-effect relationship here as Ms. Kulsoom called Mr. Waleed deserter. This serves as the cause for the later apology(the result).

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because an apology wouldn't typically cause someone to be labeled a deserter. The sequence of events is crucial here.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This implies that the two actions happened without any connection. However, the scenario strongly suggests a cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: This suggests a third, unmentioned factor caused both the labeling and the apology. While possible, it's a more complex interpretation and less directly supported by the given information. The simpler and more direct explanation is that the labeling led to the apology.

questions.explanation: Labeling someone a deserter (a serious accusation) could likely lead to consequences or social pressure. Offering an apology suggests that Ms. Kulsoom recognized her action was wrong or caused harm, and she's trying to rectify the situation. Thus, the initial action (labeling) is the cause, and the apology is a direct result (the effect).

questions.text: Statements: I. The State Bank of Pakistan has recently put restrictions on few small banks in the country. II. The small banks in the private and cooperative sector in Pakistan are not in a position to withstand the competitions of the bigger banks in the public sector.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: If the restrictions were the cause, they would have led to the small banks' inability to compete.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The inability of small banks to compete is likely why the State Bank imposed restrictions.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This would mean each statement causes separate outcomes, which is not supported by the context.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This suggests each statement results from different causes, which is inconsistent with the given information.

questions.explanation: The financial instability and inability to compete (Statement II) is a serious problem that logically cause the central banking authority to intervene and place restrictions (Statement I) to protect the financial system and depositors.

questions.text: Statement: Most of the steel producing companies in the country have made considerable profit during the last financial year. Many Asian countries have been importing huge quantities of steel from Pakistan
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because statement II does not directly cause statement I. While importing more steel can influence profits, the statements may both be effects of a third factor, such as increased global demand.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the profitability of steel companies does not directly cause an increase in steel imports by Asian countries.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This is incorrect because there is a logical connection between the two statements suggesting they are not entirely independent.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: This is correct. Both statements are likely effects of increased global demand for steel, causing both more imports from Pakistan and higher profits for steel companies.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that both statements I and II are effects of some common cause. Statement I talks about steel companies making profit, which is likely the effect of the cause mentioned in statement II - many Asian countries importing steel from Pakistan. The explanation shows how the import of steel from Pakistan leads to increased profits for the country's steel companies. The other options are incorrect as they do not capture the cause-effect relationship between the statements accurately.

questions.text: Statement I: The university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of all phones inside the college premises.Statement II: The majority of the teachers of the colleges signed a joint petition to the university complaining about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect.Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect: This is reversed. The university's action is a reaction, not an initial cause.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Since the teachers of the colleges complained to the university about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms, the university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of cell phones inside the college premises.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect. Both statements I and II are independent causes: The statements are clearly linked. The teachers' complaint directly led to the university's response. They are not independent of one another.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect. The correct answer is: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.Here's why: * Statement II: The teachers' petition highlights a problem (cell phone disturbances). * Statement I: The university's ban is a direct response to this problem; it's an action taken to address the teachers' concerns.

questions.explanation: Since the teachers of the colleges complained to the university about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms, the university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of cell phones inside the college premises.

questions.explanation: Since the government has imported large quantities of sugar as per trade agreements with other countries, therefore, the prices of sugar in the domestic market have fallen sharply in recent months.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: This assumes that the movie's financial success directly led to its Oscar nomination.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: This implies that the Oscar nomination directly caused the movie to achieve high box-office earnings.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This suggests that each statement has its own distinct cause and is not influenced by the other.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This indicates that each statement is a consequence of separate causes with no shared factors.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: The movie's box-office success and Oscar nomination can both result from common factors like its quality, effective marketing, or star cast, leading to both commercial and critical success.

questions.explanation: Statement I reflects the movie's commercial success, while Statement II highlights its critical acclaim through an Oscar nomination. Both outcomes likely stem from common factors such as the movie's quality, effective promotion, or star appeal, which contribute to both high box-office revenue and an Oscar nomination. Thus, Option E is correct. Options A and B are incorrect because neither statement directly results in the other. Option C is incorrect since the statements are not independent causes. Option D is incorrect because both statements likely share a common cause rather than having separate ones.

questions.text: Verbal Reasoning:Statements:(I) He have got cavity in his tooth(II) He eats too much chocolates
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement 1 is the cause and 2 is its effect
options.explanation: a) Statement 1 is the cause and 2 is its effect.This option suggests that statement 1, "He has got a cavity in his tooth," is the cause, and statement 2, "He eats too much chocolates," is its effect. According to this option, the reason for the cavity in his tooth is that he eats too much chocolate, and that is leading to dental issues. However, this option is not logically valid because there is no direct cause-and-effect relationship established between eating chocolates and developing a cavity. While excessive consumption of sugary foods, including chocolates, can contribute to tooth decay, it is not the only cause, and there can be other factors contributing to the cavity as well.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause and 1 is its effect
options.explanation: b) Statement 2 is the cause and 1 is its effect.This option suggests that statement 2, "He eats too much chocolates," is the cause, and statement 1, "He has got a cavity in his tooth," is its effect. Similar to the explanation in option a, this option has a direct cause-and-effect relationship between eating chocolates and developing a cavity. Excessive chocolate consumption can contribute to dental issues.

options.text: Both statements are independent causes
options.explanation: c) Both statements are independent causes.This option suggests that both statement 1 and statement 2 are independent causes, meaning they are unrelated to each other. According to this option, the person may have a cavity in his tooth for reasons other than eating chocolates excessively. Additionally, the person's habit of eating too much chocolate may not necessarily cause the cavity.

options.text: Both statements are of common cause
options.explanation: d) Both statements are of common cause.This option suggests that both statement 1 and statement 2 have a common underlying cause that leads to both conditions. In other words, there is a shared factor that contributes to the person having a cavity in his tooth and eating too much chocolate. However, this option is not logically valid because the two statements are not necessarily connected by a common cause. It is possible for a person to have a cavity for reasons unrelated to their chocolate consumption.

questions.explanation: c) Both statements are independent causes.This option suggests that both statement 1 and statement 2 are independent causes, meaning they are unrelated to each other. According to this option, the person may have a cavity in his tooth for reasons other than eating chocolates excessively. Additionally, the person's habit of eating too much chocolate may not necessarily cause the cavity. Therefore, the two statements are considered independent causes in this option.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. The State Bank of Pakistan has recently put restrictions on few small banks in the country.II. The small banks in the private and co-operative sector in Pakistan are not in a position to withstand the competitions of the bigger in the public sector.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: Statement I would be the cause if the restrictions led directly to the small banks’ inability to compete, but this is not the case here.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: Correct. Statement II indicates that small banks are struggling to compete, which likely led to the State Bank imposing restrictions to stabilize the banking sector.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: For this to be correct, the statements would not influence each other and would originate from separate issues, which is not true here.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This option would be correct if the statements were results of two unrelated causes, but they are interconnected.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause;
options.explanation: This would imply a singular underlying issue affecting both statements, but here, statement II directly leads to statement I.

questions.explanation: Statement II describes a situation where small banks are struggling to compete with larger public sector banks, which indicates a potential instability or inefficiency in their operations. This scenario likely prompted the State Bank of Pakistan to impose restrictions as described in Statement I, to manage the risk and ensure financial stability within the sector. Thus, statement II is the cause, and statement I is the effect. Options C, D, and E are incorrect as they represent scenarios where the statements are either independent or share a common cause not evident in this case.

questions.text: Statements:I. There is an unprecedented increase in the number of young unemployed in comparison to the previous year.II. A large number of candidates submitted applications against an advertisement for the post of manager issued by a bank.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it suggests that the increase in the number of young unemployed individuals (Statement I) is the cause, and the large number of job applications for the bank manager position (Statement II) is the effect. However, there is no logical or causal relationship between the two statements. They describe separate situations without any indication that one is causing the other.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it suggests that both the increase in the number of young unemployed individuals (Statement I) and the large number of job applications for the bank manager position (Statement II) are independent causes of something else. However, there is no context or information provided in the given statements to infer any relationship between these two scenarios or any external effect they might cause.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements I and II are not the effects of independent causes because each statement describes different situations with their separate underlying factors or causes.

questions.explanation: An increase in the number of unemployed youth is bound to draw in huge crowds for a single vacancy.

questions.text: Statements: I. The dialogue between Jinnah and Gandhi failed. II. Indian National Congress and Muslim League tried to join hands, so they will be united.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This interpretation suggests that the specific failure of the top two leaders to reach an agreement (Statement I) created a crisis that forced the broader political parties (Congress and League) to attempt a formal alliance or “join hands” (Statement II) as a desperate secondary measure to save the independence movement.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This logic implies that because the two political institutions were already struggling or failing in their attempt to “join hands” or unite (Statement II), it directly led to the collapse and failure of the personal dialogue between their respective leaders, Jinnah and Gandhi (Statement I).

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: The failure of the dialogue between Jinnah and Gandhi can be viewed as an independent event caused by deep-seated personal ideological differences, conflicting visions for constitutional safeguards, or specific disagreements on the formula for power-sharing. The attempt by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League to “join hands” represents a separate strategic initiative, likely driven by the external pressure of British colonial policy or a broad desire for a unified front against imperial rule, rather than being a direct reaction to the specific failure mentioned in Statement I.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: This option suggests that both the failure of the leaders (Statement I) and the attempts by the parties to unite (Statement II) were separate results of a third, unmentioned factor, such as “British Divide and Rule policy” or “extreme communal tension”. In this view, neither statement causes the other; they are both simply symptoms of a larger, different problem.

questions.explanation: Gandhi and Jinnah were unable to reach a decision regarding Pakistan hence the dialogue failed. In order to show a united front to the British as they believe that would help them achieve independence, the INC and ML tried to join hands(work together). Thus both these statements are effects of independent causes.

Statement I talks about steel companies making profit, which is likely the effect of the cause mentioned in statement II - many Asian countries importing steel from Pakistan. The explanation shows how the import of steel from Pakistan leads to increased profits for the country's steel companies. The other options are incorrect as they do not capture the cause-effect relationship between the statements accurately.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes. Statement I: Most of the "steel-producing" companies in the country have made a considerable profit during the last financial year. Statement II: Many "Asian countries" have been importing huge quantities of steel from Pakistan.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes. I. It was raining cats and dogs after a week of heatwave in Karachi. II. The weather forecast predicts pleasant weather for next week.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is wrong because I is not the cause and II is not the effect. They both are independent effects of some independent causes.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is wrong because II is not the cause and I is not the effects. Both statements are independent effects.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: This option is also wrong because both statements are not independent causes. They are independent effects.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause.
options.explanation: Both statements are independent effects of the same independent causes because pleasant weather refers clear to the sky or warm weather while on the other hand, heavy shower after a heatwave is the opposite to it. So, both are independent effects.

questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because I is not the cause and II is not the effect. They both are independent effects of some independent causes. Option B is wrong because II is not the cause and I is not the effects. Both statements are independent effects. Option C is also wrong because both statements are not independent causes. They are independent effects. Option : D Both statements are independent effects of the same independent causes because pleasant weather refers to clear sky or warm weather while on the other hand, a heavy shower after a heatwave is the opposite to it. So, both are independent effects.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

questions.text: Statements: I. The State Government has announced a special tax package for the new industries to be set up in the state. II. Last year the State Government had hiked the taxes for all industrial activities in the State.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This would imply that the special tax package led to an increase in taxes last year, which is logically inconsistent.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The tax hike last year may have deterred industrial growth, prompting the government to introduce a special tax package to attract new industries.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This assumes that both statements are causes of different effects, but they are related as a cause and effect.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This suggests that each statement is an effect of a different cause; however, statement II logically leads to statement I.

questions.explanation: A tax hike (Statement II) could have discouraged new investment or caused economic slowdown. A special tax package for new industries (Statement I) would be a logical policy response to counteract the negative effects of the previous year's tax hike.

questions.text: Statements: I. More than two billion pounds of chemicals are spewed in the air everyday by industries and vehicles of some countries II. International pollution control organisation is expected to penalise the countries which cross the maximum emission limits.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I describes the excessive emission of pollutants, which, in turn, leads to international actions such as penalties mentioned in Statement II.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the imposition of penalties does not lead to pollution; rather, it is a response to it.

options.text: Both Statement I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: While both statements address pollution, Statement II is not an independent cause but a response to the situation described in Statement I.

options.text: Both Statement I and II are effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: This is incorrect as there is a clear cause-effect relationship between the two statements, with the pollution in Statement I leading to the actions in Statement II.

questions.explanation: The massive scale of pollution (Statement I) is the problem or cause. The expected penalty from a control organization (Statement II) is the logical consequence or effect aimed at addressing this problem.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. The university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban use of all phones inside the college premises.II. Majority of the teachers of the colleges signed a joint petition to the university complaining the disturbances caused by cell phone ring-tones inside the classrooms.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: This would mean the university's decision to ban phones led teachers to complain, which is not logical.

options.text: If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;
options.explanation: The teachers' petition about disturbances caused by cell phone ring-tones likely prompted the university to ban phones.

options.text: If both the statements I and ll are independent causes;
options.explanation: This would mean both statements happened for unrelated reasons, which is unlikely given the context.

options.text: If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This would mean both outcomes arose from different causes, but the context suggests a connection.

options.text: If both the statements I and II are effeets of some common cause;
options.explanation: This would mean there is an underlying reason causing both events, but the direct link between the petition and the ban is more plausible.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The teachers' petition (Statement II) highlights a problem with cell phone disturbances in classrooms, which likely led the university to take action by banning phones (Statement I) to solve this issue. Option A is incorrect because the ban cannot cause the teachers to complain about an existing issue. Option C is incorrect because the statements are related, not independent. Option D is incorrect as the statements are not effects of independent causes; they are connected. Option E is incorrect because there is no need to assume a separate common cause when the petition itself is a sufficient reason for the ban.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. The government has taken the new initiafive in which the fee charged by the unaided institutions has been reduced as compared to last year.II. The students have protested against the high fee last year.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: This would mean that the government's initiative to reduce fees led to student protests, which is illogical.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: This suggests that student protests against high fees last year prompted the government to reduce fees this year.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: This implies that both statements happened independently without influencing each other, which is unlikely given the context.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: This suggests that both are results of different, unrelated causes, which doesn't fit the given scenario.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;
options.explanation: This would mean both the protest and the fee reduction were due to a common cause, which is not suggested by the statements.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B. Statement II is the cause, and statement I is its effect. The students' protests against high fees last year likely influenced the government's decision to reduce fees this year. Option A is incorrect because the initiative to reduce fees could not have caused last year's protests. Option C is incorrect, as both statements are not independent causes; they are connected. Option D is incorrect because the statements are not effects of different causes; they are related. Option E is incorrect because there is no indication of a common cause for both statements beyond the protest influencing the fee reduction.
